title: Express Shipping Address Injection Prior to High-Value Electronics Purchase
narrative: Account taken over via credential stuffing shows immediate address book manipulation followed by rapid high-value purchase. Attacker logs in, adds new shipping address (dist1 >1000km from previous addr1), then within 45-90 minutes places $1,200-$2,800 order for electronics (ProductCD='W') or gift cards (ProductCD='C'). Original account holder's R_emaildomain remains unchanged but shipping addr2 now differs from billing addr1. Transaction velocity spikes from baseline 0.8 txns/week to 3+ transactions in single session. Card_txn_count_24hr jumps from 0 to 4-6 as attacker tests limits. No prior purchases to new addr1 in account history. Expedited shipping selected despite account showing 18+ months of standard shipping preference. Purchase occurs during is_night=1 hours when legitimate user historically transacts during business hours.
indicators: dist1 exceeds 1000km between new shipping addr1 and historical billing address
Address added to account 45-90 minutes before TransactionAmt $1,200-$2,800 purchase
card1_txn_count_24hr increases from 0 to 4-6 transactions in single session
ProductCD='W' (electronics) or ProductCD='C' (gift cards) with expedited shipping flag
txn_hour during is_night=1 contradicts 18+ month history of daytime transactions
addr2 (shipping) differs from addr1 (billing) for first time in account tenure
card1_amt_zscore exceeds 3.5 standard deviations above 90-day account baseline
summary: Account shows a new shipping address added 1000+ km away from billing, followed within 45-90 minutes by a high-value electronics or gift card purchase with expedited shipping during nighttime hours—contradicting 18+ months of daytime, standard-shipping behavior.
reasoning: The attacker gained access through credential stuffing and immediately inserted a distant drop address to intercept expensive goods before the legitimate owner notices, using express shipping to minimize detection window and choosing resellable items to quickly monetize the fraud.
indicator_explanations: Shipping address is over 1000km away from the cardholder's billing location
New address appears on account less than two hours before expensive purchase
Card activity spikes from zero to multiple transactions in one session
High-value electronics or gift cards ordered with rush delivery
Purchase happens at night when cardholder normally shops during the day
Items shipping to different address than billing for first time ever
Purchase amount drastically exceeds the account's normal spending pattern
